name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.api.dto.AddressDto
comment: The AddressDto represents the message payload to change a customer's address. This is an example of the Atomic Parameter List pattern.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.api.dto.CitiesResponseDto
comment: The CitiesResponseDto represents a list of city names, transferred as simple (atomic) strings. This is an example of the Atomic Parameter pattern.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.api.dto.CustomerNotFoundException
comment: You might be wondering why this Exception class is in the dtos package. Exceptions of this type are thrown whenever the client sends a request with an invalid customer id. Spring will then convert this exception into an HTTP 404 response.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.api.dto.CustomerProfileUpdateRequestDto
comment: CustomerProfileUpdateRequestDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents the personal data (customer profile) of a customer. It is sent to the CustomerInformationHolder when a new customer is created or the profile of an existing customer is updated.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.api.dto.CustomerResponseDto
comment: The CustomerResponseDto represents a customer, including their complete move history. This is an example of the Parameter Tree pattern.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.api.dto.CustomersResponseDto
comment: The CustomersResponseDto holds a collection of @CustomerResponseDto Parameter Trees. This class is an example of the Parameter Forest pattern.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.api.dto.PaginatedCustomerResponseDto
comment: The PaginatedCustomerResponseDto holds a collection of CustomerResponseDto with additional control Metadata Elements such as the limit, offset and size that are used in the Pagination pattern, specifically the Offset-Based Pagination variant.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.application.CustomerService
comment: The CustomerService class is an application service that is responsible for creating, updating and retrieving customer entities.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.application.DataLoader
comment: The run() method of the DataLoader class is automatically executed when the application launches. It populates the database with sample customers that can be used to test the application.

methods.name: loadCustomers
methods.comment: Note: The files mock_customers_small.csv and mock_customers_large.csv are the same as the files mock_users_small.csv and mock_users_large.csv in the Customer Self-Service backend. The Customer Core DataLoader creates a customer for each row in the corresponding CSV file and the Customer Self-Service DataLoader creates a user login for each of these customers. Therefore, these files should be kept in sync.  This dummy data was generated using https://mockaroo.com/

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.application.Page
comment: An instance of the Page class can be used to represent a subset of a specific type of resource.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.domain.city.CityLookupService
comment: This is a DDD Domain Service and is automatically injected thanks to the @Component annotation. Note that we could also have used the @Service annotation, which is an alias for @Component.

methods.name: getCitiesForPostalCode
methods.comment: Returns an alphabetically ordered list of cities that match the given postal code.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.domain.customer.Address
comment: An Address is a value object that is used to represent the postal address of a customer.  You might be wondering why the Address class implements the ValueObject interface even though it has a JPA @Entity annotation. This discrepancy exists for technical reasons. JPA requires Address to be declared as an entity, because it is part of a one-to-many relationship. However, in the DDD sense, Address behaves like a Value Object (i.e., it has no id and is immutable).

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.domain.customer.CustomerAggregateRoot
comment: CustomerAggregateRoot is the root entity of the Customer aggregate. Note that there is no class for the Customer aggregate, so the package can be seen as aggregate.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.domain.customer.CustomerFactory
comment: CustomerFactory is a factory that is responsible for the creation of CustomerAggregateRoot objects. It makes sure that each new customer has a unique CustomerId and that phone numbers are formatted correctly.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.domain.customer.CustomerId
comment: A CustomerId is a value object that is used to represent the unique id of a customer.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.domain.customer.CustomerProfileEntity
comment: CustomerProfileEntity is an entity that contains the personal data (customer profile) of a CustomerAggregateRoot.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.infrastructure.CustomerRepository
comment: The CustomerRepository can be used to read and write CustomerAggregateRoot objects from and to the backing database. Spring automatically searches for interfaces that extend the JpaRepository interface and creates a corresponding Spring bean for each of them. For more information on repositories visit the Spring Data JPA - Reference Documentation.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.interfaces.CityReferenceDataHolder
comment: This REST controller allows clients to retrieve a list of cities that match a given postal code. It is an application of the Reference Data Holder pattern. A Reference Data Holder is a dedicated endpoint that serves as single point of reference for static data (i.e., data that almost never changes).

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.interfaces.CustomerInformationHolder
comment: This REST controller gives clients access to the customer data. It is an example of the Information Holder Resource pattern. This particular one is a special type of information holder called Master Data Holder.

methods.name: getCustomers
methods.comment:
Returns a 'page' of customers.

The query parameters {@code limit} and {@code offset} can be used to specify the maximum size of the page and the offset of the page's first customer. Example Usage: 
  GET http://localhost:8110/customers?limit=2&offset=2  { "limit" : 2, "offset" : 2, "size" : 50, "customers" : [ { "customerId" : "ctkdzorjl0", "firstname" : "Rahel", "lastname" : "Piletic", ... }, { "customerId" : "5xvivyzxvc", "firstname" : "Cullin", "lastname" : "Manske", ... } ], "_links" : { "self" : { "href" : "http://localhost:8110/customers?limit=2&offset=2&fields=" }, "prev" : { "href" : "http://localhost:8110/customers?limit=2&offset=0&fields=" }, "next" : { "href" : "http://localhost:8110/customers?limit=2&offset=4&fields=" } } }
The response contains the customers, limit and offset of the current page as well as the total number of customers (size). Additionally, it contains HATEOAS-style links that link to the endpoint address of the current, previous and next page.

methods.name: getCustomer
methods.comment:
Returns the customers for the given customer ids.

The client can provide a comma-separated list of customer ids to fetch a particular set of customers. This is a variant of the Request Bundle pattern: 
  GET http://localhost:8110/customers/ce4btlyluu,rgpp0wkpec  { "customers" : [ { "customerId" : "ce4btlyluu", "firstname" : "Robbie", "lastname" : "Davenhall", "birthday" : "1961-08-11T23:00:00.000+0000", ... "_links" : { ... } }, { "customerId" : "rgpp0wkpec", "firstname" : "Max", "lastname" : "Mustermann", "birthday" : "1989-12-31T23:00:00.000+0000", ... "_links" : { ... } } ], "_links" : { "self" : { "href" : "http://localhost:8110/customers/ce4btlyluu,rgpp0wkpec?fields=" } } }
By default getCustomer() returns a response that includes all fields. The client can also reduce the size of the response by providing a so-called Wish List with the query parameter {@code fields}: 
  GET http://localhost:8110/customers/ce4btlyluu,rgpp0wkpec?fields=firstname,lastname  { "customers" : [ { "firstname" : "Robbie", "lastname" : "Davenhall", "_links" : { ... } }, { "firstname" : "Max", "lastname" : "Mustermann", "_links" : { ... } } ], "_links" : { "self" : { "href" : "http://localhost:8110/customers/ce4btlyluu,rgpp0wkpec?fields=" } } }

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.interfaces.ErrorController
comment: This class implements a custom error controller that returns an Error Report.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.interfaces.OldCustomerInformationHolder
comment: This controller demonstrates the use of a 301 Moved Permanently response to redirect requests to a new URI. See the Rename Endpoint refactoring for more information.

methods.name: getCustomer
methods.comment: Handles GET requests for customer information.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.interfaces.configuration.APIKeyAuthFilter
comment: This class extracts the API Key from the request's HTTP header.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.interfaces.configuration.APIKeyAuthenticationManager
comment:
The APIKeyAuthenticationManager ensures that only known clients can access the Customer Core API. It is an example of the API Key pattern where each client identifies itself with an API Key. Example:

Missing API Key 
  $ curl localhost:8110/customers { "timestamp" : "2018-11-12T15:39:14.959+0000", "status" : 403, "error" : "Forbidden", "message" : "Access Denied", "path" : "/customers" }
Valid API Key 
  $ curl -H "Authorization: Bearer b318ad736c6c844b" localhost:8110/customers { "filter" : "", "limit" : 10, "offset" : 0, "size" : 50, "customers" : [ 	... ] }

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.interfaces.configuration.RequestLoggingFilterConfig
comment: This filter enabled logging on all requests. See this blog post for details.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.interfaces.configuration.SwaggerConfiguration
comment: The SwaggerConfiguration class configures the HTTP resource API documentation.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.interfaces.configuration.WebConfiguration
comment: The WebConfiguration class is used to customize the default Spring MVC configuration.

methods.name: h2servletRegistration
methods.comment: This web servlet makes the web console of the H2 database engine available at the "/console" endpoint.

methods.name: shallowETagHeaderFilter
methods.comment: This is a filter that generates an ETag value based on the content of the response. This ETag is compared to the If-None-Match header of the request. If these headers are equal, the response content is not sent, but rather a 304 "Not Modified" status instead.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.interfaces.configuration.WebSecurityConfiguration
comment: The WebSecurityConfiguration class configures the security policies used for the exposed HTTP resource API. In this case, it ensures that only clients with a valid API key can access the API.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.interfaces.converters.StringToCustomerIdConverter
comment: This converter class allows us to use CustomerId as the type of a @PathVariable parameter in a Spring @RestController class.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.interfaces.dtos.city.CitiesResponseDto

comment: The AddressDto represents the message payload to change a customer's address. This is an example of the Atomic Parameter List pattern.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.interfaces.dtos.customer.CustomerNotFoundException
comment: You might be wondering why this Exception class is in the dtos package. Exceptions of this type are thrown whenever the client sends a request with an invalid customer id. Spring will then convert this exception into an HTTP 404 response.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.interfaces.dtos.customer.CustomerProfileUpdateRequestDto
comment: CustomerProfileUpdateRequestDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents the personal data (customer profile) of a customer. It is sent to the CustomerInformationHolder when a new customer is created or the profile of an existing customer is updated.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.interfaces.dtos.customer.CustomerResponseDto
comment: The CustomerResponseDto represents a customer, including their complete move history. This is an example of the Parameter Tree pattern.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.interfaces.dtos.customer.CustomersResponseDto

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.interfaces.validation.PhoneNumber
comment: This @interface declaration defines a custom @PhoneNumber annotation which can be used to validate phone numbers in a request DTO. Note that the PhoneNumberValidator class performs the actual validation.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customercore.interfaces.validation.PhoneNumberValidator
comment: This class validates phone numbers using Google's libphonenumber (see https://github.com/googlei18n/libphonenumber).

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.api.InsuranceQuoteRequestSubmitted
comment: Published by CustomerSelfService when a quote request is submitted. Consumed by PolicyManagement.  Part of the CustomerSelfService module public API (named interface "api").

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.api.InsuranceQuoteResponded
comment: Published by PolicyManagement after checking a quote request. Consumed by CustomerSelfService to transition REQUEST_SUBMITTED -> QUOTE_RECEIVED (or REQUEST_REJECTED).

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.api.PolicyCreated
comment: Published by PolicyManagement after creating a policy. Consumed by CustomerSelfService to transition -> POLICY_CREATED.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.application.DataLoader
comment: The run() method of the DataLoader class is automatically executed when the application launches. It populates the database with sample user logins that can be used to test the application.

methods.name: loadUsers
methods.comment: Note: The files mock_users_small.csv and mock_users_large.csv are the same as the files mock_customers_small.csv and mock_customers_large.csv in the Customer Core. The Customer Core DataLoader creates a customer for each row in the corresponding CSV file and the Customer Self-Service DataLoader creates a user login for each of these customers. Therefore, these files should be kept in sync.  This dummy data was generated using https://mockaroo.com/

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.domain.customer.Address
comment: An Address is a value object that is used to represent the postal address of a customer.  You might be wondering why the Address class implements the ValueObject interface even though it has a JPA @Entity(name = "CustomerSelfServiceCustomerAddress") annotation. This discrepancy exists for technical reasons. JPA requires Address to be declared as an entity, because it is part of a one-to-many relationship. However, in the DDD sense, Address behaves like a value object.  You also might be wondering why this code is the same as that in the Customer Core. This does violate DRY but for good reasons: strategic DD suggests that model boundaries decouple (sub-)systems so that they can be deployed and evolved independently.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.domain.customer.CustomerId
comment: A CustomerId is a value object that is used to represent the unique id of a customer.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.domain.identityaccess.UserDetailsServiceImpl
comment: UserDetailsServiceImpl is a domain service that can load user logins from the UserLoginRepository.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.domain.identityaccess.UserLoginEntity
comment: UserLogin is an entity that contains the login credentials of a specific user.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.domain.identityaccess.UserSecurityDetails
comment: UserSecurityDetails is an adapter class which makes the login credentials of a specific UserLogin available through Spring's UserDetails interface.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.domain.insurancequoterequest.CustomerDecisionEvent
comment: CustomerDecisionEvent is a domain event class that is used to notify the Policy Management Backend about a decision by a customer to accept or reject a specific Insurance Quote.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.domain.insurancequoterequest.CustomerInfoEntity
comment: CustomerInfoEntity is an entity that is part of an InsuranceQuoteRequestAggregateRoot and contains infos about the initiator of the request.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.domain.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceOptionsEntity
comment: InsuranceOptionsEntity is an entity that contains the insurance options (e.g., start date, insurance type, etc.) that a customer selected for an Insurance Quote Request.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.domain.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceQuoteEntity
comment: InsuranceQuoteEntity is an entity that represents an Insurance Quote which has been submitted as a response to a specific Insurance Quote Request.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.domain.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceQuoteExpiredEvent
comment: InsuranceQuoteExpiredEvent is a domain event class that is used to notify the Customer Self-Service Backend when the Insurance Quote for a specific Insurance Quote Request has expired.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.domain.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceQuoteRequestAggregateRoot
comment: InsuranceQuoteRequestAggregateRoot is the root entity of the Insurance Quote Request aggregate. Note that there is no class for the Insurance Quote Request aggregate, so the package can be seen as aggregate.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.domain.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceQuoteRequestEvent
comment: InsuranceQuoteRequestEvent is a domain event class that is used to notify the Policy Management Backend when a new Insurance Quote Request has been submitted by a customer.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.domain.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceQuoteResponseEvent
comment: InsuranceQuoteResponseEvent is a domain event class that is used to notify the Customer Self-Service Backend when Lakeside Mutual has submitted a response for a specific Insurance Quote Request.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.domain.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceType
comment: An instance of InsuranceType is a value object that is used to represent the type of insurance (e.g., health insurance, life insurance, etc).

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.domain.insurancequoterequest.MoneyAmount
comment: An instance of MoneyAmount is a value object that represents an amount of money in a specific currency. For example, this is used to represent the insurance premium of a policy.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.domain.insurancequoterequest.PolicyCreatedEvent
comment: PolicyCreatedEvent is a domain event class that is used to notify the Customer Self-Service Backend when a new Policy has been created after an Insurance Quote has been accepted.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.domain.insurancequoterequest.RequestStatus
comment:
A RequestStatus is a value object that is used to represent the current status of an insurance quote request. The following diagram shows the possible state transitions: 
  │ ▼ ┌────────────┐ │  REQUEST_  │ │ SUBMITTED  │ └────────────┘ │ ┌─────────────────┴────────────────┐ │                                  │ ▼                                  ▼ ┌────────────┐                     ╔════════════╗ │   QUOTE_   │                     ║  REQUEST_  ║ ┌───────────│  RECEIVED  │─────────────┐       ║  REJECTED  ║ │           └────────────┘             │       ╚════════════╝ │                  │                   │ │                  │                   │ ▼                  ▼                   ▼ ╔════════════╗     ┌────────────┐      ╔════════════╗ ║   QUOTE_   ║     │   QUOTE_   │      ║   QUOTE_   ║ ║  REJECTED  ║     │  ACCEPTED  │─────▶║  EXPIRED   ║ ╚════════════╝     └────────────┘      ╚════════════╝ │ │ ▼ ╔════════════╗ ║  POLICY_   ║ ║  CREATED   ║ ╚════════════╝

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.infrastructure.CustomerCoreClientImpl
comment: Modulith migration adapter.  Option A (in-process) means: this bean exists so wiring succeeds, and later you replace these method bodies to call Customer Core services directly.  For now, we return 501/throw to make it explicit what is not implemented yet, while still allowing the Spring context to create the bean.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.infrastructure.CustomerCoreRemoteProxy
comment: CustomerCoreRemoteProxy is a remote proxy that interacts with the Customer Core in order to give the Customer Self-Service Backend's own clients access to the shared customer data.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.infrastructure.CustomerSelfServiceInsuranceQuoteRequestRepository
comment: The InsuranceQuoteRequestRepository can be used to read and write InsuranceQuoteRequestAggregateRoot objects from and to the backing database. Spring automatically searches for interfaces that extend the JpaRepository interface and creates a corresponding Spring bean for each of them. For more information on repositories visit the Spring Data JPA - Reference Documentation.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.infrastructure.UserLoginRepository
comment: The UserLoginRepository can be used to read and write UserLogin objects from and to the backing database. Spring automatically searches for interfaces that extend the JpaRepository interface and creates a corresponding Spring bean for each of them. For more information on repositories visit the Spring Data JPA - Reference Documentation.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.AuthenticationController
comment: This class is a REST Controller that is used to authenticate existing users and to sign up new users.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.CityReferenceDataHolder
comment: This REST controller allows clients to retrieve a list of cities that match a given postal code. It is an application of the Reference Data Holder pattern. A Reference Data Holder is a dedicated endpoint that serves as single point of reference for static data (i.e., data that almost never changes).

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.CustomerInformationHolder
comment: This REST controller gives clients access to the customer data. It is an example of the Information Holder Resource pattern. This particular one is a special type of information holder called Master Data Holder.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.ErrorController
comment: This class implements a custom error controller that returns an Error Report.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.InsuranceQuoteExpiredMessageConsumer
comment: InsuranceQuoteExpiredMessageConsumer is a Spring component that consumes InsuranceQuoteExpiredEvents as they arrive through the ActiveMQ message queue. It processes these events by marking the corresponding insurance quote requests as expired.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.InsuranceQuoteRequestCoordinator
comment: This REST controller gives clients access to the insurance quote requests. It is an example of the Information Holder Resource pattern. This particular one is a special type of information holder called Operational Data Holder.

methods.name: getInsuranceQuoteRequests
methods.comment: This endpoint is only used for debugging purposes.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.InsuranceQuoteResponseMessageConsumer
comment: InsuranceQuoteResponseMessageConsumer is a Spring component that consumes InsuranceQuoteResponseEvents as they arrive through the ActiveMQ message queue. It processes these events by updating the status of the corresponding insurance quote requests.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.PolicyCreatedMessageConsumer
comment: PolicyCreatedMessageConsumer is a Spring component that consumes PolicyCreatedEvents as they arrive through the ActiveMQ message queue. It processes these events by updating the status of the corresponding insurance quote requests.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.UserInformationHolder
comment: This REST controller gives clients access to the current user. It is an example of the Information Holder Resource pattern. This particular one is a special type of information holder called Master Data Holder.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.configuration.AuthenticationTokenFilter
comment: The AuthenticationTokenFilter class ensures that only requests with a valid JWT (JSON Web Token) get through.

methods.name: authenticationManager
methods.comment: Exposes AuthenticationManager so AuthenticationController can authenticate via the configured UserDetailsService.

methods.name: passwordEncoder
methods.comment: Matches what your signup uses (BCrypt).

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.configuration.DefaultAuthenticatedRestTemplateClient
comment: Provides a RestTemplate that can be injected into other components. The RestTemplate uses the configured API Key when making a request.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.configuration.JwtUtils
comment: JwtUtils is a utility class that lets clients generate, validate and parse JSON Web Tokens (JWT).

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.configuration.RateLimitInterceptor
comment:
RateLimitInterceptor ensures that client can't overuse the service. What follows is a simple implementation that limits clients to 60 requests per minute. Clients are identified by their IP address. Once the limit is reached, a {@code 429 Too Many Requests} response is returned. To test the limit, the Apache Bench tool ab can be used: 
 {@code  ab -n 100  http://localhost:8080/customers  }
In the output you should see that all 100 requests were answered but 40 had a Non-2xx response: 
 {@code  Complete requests:      100 Failed requests:        40 Non-2xx responses:      40  }
Note that the Rate Limiting library uses an in-memory store for the rate limits, so you can simply restart the server to reset the limit. This interceptor is configured in {@link WebConfiguration} to intercept all requests to this application.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.configuration.SwaggerConfiguration
comment: The SwaggerConfiguration class configures the HTTP resource API documentation that is generated by Springfox.

comment: The WebConfiguration class is used to customize the default Spring MVC configuration.

methods.comment: This method adds a custom interceptor to the InterceptorRegistry in order to enable rate-limiting.

methods.comment: This web servlet makes the web console of the H2 database engine available at the "/console" endpoint.

methods.name: shallowETagHeaderFilter
methods.comment: This is a filter that generates an ETag value based on the content of the response. This ETag is compared to the If-None-Match header of the request. If these headers are equal, the response content is not sent, but rather a 304 "Not Modified" status instead.

comment: The WebSecurityConfiguration class configures the security policies used for the exposed HTTP resource API. In this case, it ensures that only authenticated users can access the API.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.converters.StringToCustomerIdConverter
comment: This converter class allows us to use CustomerId as the type of a @PathVariable parameter in a Spring @RestController class.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.dtos.city.CitiesResponseDto
comment: The CitiesResponseDto represents a list of city names, transferred as simple (atomic) strings. This is an example of the Atomic Parameter pattern.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.dtos.customer.AddressDto
comment: AddressDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents the postal address of a customer.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.dtos.customer.CustomerCoreNotAvailableException
comment: You might be wondering why this Exception class is in the dtos package. Exceptions of this type are thrown whenever the Customer Self-Service backend can't connect to the Customer Core. Spring will then convert this exception into an HTTP 502 response.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.dtos.customer.CustomerDto
comment: The CustomerDto class is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents a single customer. It inherits from the ResourceSupport class which allows us to create a REST representation (e.g., JSON, XML) that follows the HATEOAS principle. For example, links can be added to the representation (e.g., self, address.change) which means that future actions the client may take can be discovered from the resource representation.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.dtos.customer.CustomerIdDto
comment: CustomerIdDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents the unique ID of a customer.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.dtos.customer.CustomerNotFoundException
comment: You might be wondering why this Exception class is in the dtos package. Exceptions of this type are thrown whenever the client sends a request with an invalid customer id. Spring will then convert this exception into an HTTP 404 response.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.dtos.customer.CustomerProfileDto
comment: CustomerProfileDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents the personal data (customer profile) of a customer.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.dtos.customer.CustomerProfileUpdateRequestDto
comment: CustomerProfileUpdateRequestDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents the personal data (customer profile) of a customer. It is sent to the Customer Core when a new customer is created.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.dtos.customer.CustomerRegistrationRequestDto
comment: CustomerRegistrationRequestDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents the personal data (customer profile) of a customer. It is sent to the CustomerInformationHolder when a user completes the registration process in the Customer Self-Service frontend.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.dtos.customer.CustomersDto
comment: The CustomersDto class is a data transfer object (DTO) that contains a list of customers. It inherits from the ResourceSupport class which allows us to create a REST representation (e.g., JSON, XML) that follows the HATEOAS principle. For example, links can be added to the representation (e.g., self, next, prev) which means that future actions the client may take can be discovered from the resource representation.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.dtos.identityaccess.AuthenticationRequestDto
comment: AuthenticationRequestDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents the login credentials of a user. It is sent to the AuthenticationController when a user tries to log into the Customer Self-Service frontend.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.dtos.identityaccess.AuthenticationResponseDto
comment: AuthenticationResponseDto is a data transfer object (DTO) which contains the JWT token that is sent to a user after he or she successfully logs into the Customer Self-Service frontend.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.dtos.identityaccess.SignupRequestDto
comment: SignupRequestDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents the login credentials of a new user. It is sent to the AuthenticationController when a new user tries to sign up in the Customer Self-Service frontend.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.dtos.identityaccess.UserAlreadyExistsException
comment: You might be wondering why this Exception class is in the dtos package. Exceptions of this type are thrown whenever the client tries to create an account for a user that already exists. Spring will then convert this exception into an HTTP 409 response.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.dtos.identityaccess.UserResponseDto
comment: UserResponseDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents a user. The customerId property references the customer object in the Customer Core that belongs to this user. A customerId that is set to null indicates that the user has not yet completed the registration.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.dtos.insurancequoterequest.CustomerInfoDto
comment: CustomerInfoDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents the customer infos that are part of an Insurance Quote Request.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.dtos.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceOptionsDto
comment: InsuranceOptionsDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that contains the insurance options (e.g., start date, insurance type, etc.) that a customer selected for an Insurance Quote Request.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.dtos.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceQuoteDto
comment: InsuranceQuoteDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents an Insurance Quote which has been submitted as a response to a specific Insurance Quote Request.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.dtos.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceQuoteRequestDto
comment: InsuranceQuoteRequestDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents a request by a customer for a new insurance quote.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.dtos.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceQuoteRequestNotFoundException
comment: You might be wondering why this Exception class is in the dtos package. Exceptions of this type are thrown whenever the client tries to fetch an insurance quote request that doesn't exist. Spring will then convert this exception into an HTTP 404 response.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.dtos.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceQuoteResponseDto
comment: InsuranceQuoteResponseDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that contains Lakeside Mutual's response to a specific insurance quote request.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.dtos.insurancequoterequest.MoneyAmountDto
comment: MoneyAmountDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents an amount of money in a specific currency.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.dtos.insurancequoterequest.RequestStatusChangeDto
comment: RequestStatusChangeDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents a status change of an insurance quote request.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.validation.PhoneNumber
comment: This @interface declaration defines a custom @PhoneNumber annotation which can be used to validate phone numbers in a request DTO. Note that the PhoneNumberValidator class performs the actual validation.

name: com.lakesidemutual.customerselfservice.interfaces.validation.PhoneNumberValidator
comment: This class validates phone numbers using Google's libphonenumber (see https://github.com/googlei18n/libphonenumber).

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.application.DataLoader
comment: The run() method of the DataLoader class is automatically executed when the application launches. It populates the database with sample policies that can be used to test the application.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.application.ExpirationCheckerJob
comment: ExpirationCheckerJob is a Quartz job that periodically checks for expired insurance quotes. For each expired insurance quote, it also sends an InsuranceQuoteExpiredEvent to the Customer Self-Service backend.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.application.ExpirationCheckerJobConfiguration
comment: The ExpirationCheckerJobConfiguration class is used to set up the scheduler to periodically execute the ExpirationCheckerJob.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.domain.insurancequoterequest.Address
comment: An Address is a value object that is used to represent the postal address of a customer.  You might be wondering why the Address class implements the ValueObject interface even though it has a JPA @Entity(name = "PolicyManagementAddress") annotation. This discrepancy exists for technical reasons. JPA requires Address to be declared as an entity, because it is part of a one-to-many relationship. However, in the DDD sense, Address behaves like a value object.  You also might be wondering why this code is the same as that in the Customer Core. This does violate DRY but for good reasons: strategic DD suggests that model boundaries decouple (sub-)systems so that they can be deployed and evolved independently.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.domain.insurancequoterequest.CustomerDecisionEvent
comment: CustomerDecisionEvent is a domain event class that is used to notify the Policy Management Backend about a decision by a customer to accept or reject a specific Insurance Quote.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.domain.insurancequoterequest.CustomerInfoEntity
comment: CustomerInfoEntity is an entity that is part of an InsuranceQuoteRequestAggregateRoot and contains infos about the initiator of the request.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.domain.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceOptionsEntity
comment: InsuranceOptionsEntity is an entity that contains the insurance options (e.g., start date, insurance type, etc.) that a customer selected for an Insurance Quote Request.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.domain.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceQuoteEntity
comment: InsuranceQuoteEntity is an entity that represents an Insurance Quote which has been submitted as a response to a specific Insurance Quote Request.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.domain.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceQuoteExpiredEvent
comment: InsuranceQuoteExpiredEvent is a domain event class that is used to notify the Customer Self-Service Backend when the Insurance Quote for a specific Insurance Quote Request has expired.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.domain.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceQuoteRequestAggregateRoot
comment: InsuranceQuoteRequestAggregateRoot is the root entity of the Insurance Quote Request aggregate. Note that there is no class for the Insurance Quote Request aggregate, so the package can be seen as aggregate.

methods.name: checkQuoteExpirationDate
methods.comment: Checks the quote expiration date and changes the request status to QUOTE_EXPIRED if necessary. Returns true if the request status has been changed.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.domain.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceQuoteRequestEvent
comment: InsuranceQuoteRequestEvent is a domain event class that is used to notify the Policy Management Backend when a new Insurance Quote Request has been submitted by a customer.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.domain.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceQuoteResponseEvent
comment: InsuranceQuoteResponseEvent is a domain event class that is used to notify the Customer Self-Service Backend when Lakeside Mutual has submitted a response for a specific Insurance Quote Request.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.domain.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceType
comment: An instance of InsuranceType is a value object that is used to represent the type of insurance (e.g., health insurance, life insurance, etc).

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.domain.insurancequoterequest.PolicyCreatedEvent
comment: PolicyCreatedEvent is a domain event class that is used to notify the Customer Self-Service Backend when a new Policy has been created after an Insurance Quote has been accepted.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.domain.insurancequoterequest.RequestStatus
comment:
A RequestStatus is a value object that is used to represent the current status of an insurance quote request. The following diagram shows the possible state transitions: 
  │ ▼ ┌────────────┐ │  REQUEST_  │ │ SUBMITTED  │ └────────────┘ │ ┌─────────────────┴────────────────┐ │                                  │ ▼                                  ▼ ┌────────────┐                     ╔════════════╗ │   QUOTE_   │                     ║  REQUEST_  ║ ┌───────────│  RECEIVED  │─────────────┐       ║  REJECTED  ║ │           └────────────┘             │       ╚════════════╝ │                  │                   │ │                  │                   │ ▼                  ▼                   ▼ ╔════════════╗     ┌────────────┐      ╔════════════╗ ║   QUOTE_   ║     │   QUOTE_   │      ║   QUOTE_   ║ ║  REJECTED  ║     │  ACCEPTED  │─────▶║  EXPIRED   ║ ╚════════════╝     └────────────┘      ╚════════════╝ │ │ ▼ ╔════════════╗ ║  POLICY_   ║ ║  CREATED   ║ ╚════════════╝

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.domain.insurancequoterequest.RequestStatusChange
comment: An instance of RequestStatusChange is a value object that represents a status change of an insurance quote request. It contains the date of the status change as well as the new status.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.domain.policy.DeletePolicyEvent
comment: DeletePolicyEvent is a domain event that is sent to the Risk Management Server every time a policy is deleted.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.domain.policy.InsuringAgreementEntity
comment: InsuringAgreementEntity is an entity which represents the insuring agreement between a customer and Lakeside Mutual. Each InsuringAgreementEntity belongs to a PolicyAggregateRoot.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.domain.policy.InsuringAgreementItem
comment: An InsuranceAgreementItem is a value object that is used to represent a single item in an insuring agreement.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.domain.policy.MoneyAmount
comment: An instance of MoneyAmount is a value object that represents an amount of money in a specific currency. For example, this is used to represent the insurance premium of a policy.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.domain.policy.PolicyAggregateRoot
comment: PolicyAggregateRoot is the root entity of the Policy aggregate. Note that there is no class for the Policy aggregate, so the package can be seen as aggregate.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.domain.policy.PolicyId
comment: A PolicyId is a value object that is used to represent the unique id of a policy.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.domain.policy.PolicyPeriod
comment: A PolicyPeriod is a value object that is used to represent the period during which a policy is valid.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.domain.policy.PolicyType
comment: A PolicyType is a value object that is used to represent the type of a policy (e.g., health insurance, life insurance, etc).

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.domain.policy.UpdatePolicyEvent
comment: UpdatePolicyEvent is a domain event that is sent to the Risk Management Server every time a new policy is created or an existing policy is updated.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.infrastructure.CustomerSelfServiceMessageProducer
comment: CustomerSelfServiceMessageProducer is an infrastructure service class that is used to notify the Customer Self-Service Backend when Lakeside Mutual has responded to a customer's insurance quote request (InsuranceQuoteResponseEvent) or when an insurance quote has expired (InsuranceQuoteExpiredEvent). These events are transmitted via an ActiveMQ message queue.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.infrastructure.InProcessCustomerCoreClient
comment: Replaces CustomerCoreRemoteProxy (HTTP). Uses customercore::api in-process.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.infrastructure.PolicyInsuranceQuoteRequestRepository
comment: The InsuranceQuoteRequestRepository can be used to read and write InsuranceQuoteRequestAggregateRoot objects from and to the backing database. Spring automatically searches for interfaces that extend the JpaRepository interface and creates a corresponding Spring bean for each of them. For more information on repositories visit the Spring Data JPA - Reference Documentation.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.infrastructure.PolicyRepository
comment: The PolicyRepository can be used to read and write PolicyAggregateRoot objects from and to the backing database. Spring automatically searches for interfaces that extend the JpaRepository interface and creates a corresponding Spring bean for each of them. For more information on repositories visit the Spring Data JPA - Reference Documentation.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.infrastructure.RiskManagementMessageProducer
comment: RiskManagementMessageProducer is an infrastructure service class that is used to notify the Risk Management Server about policy events (e.g., a new policy is created). These events are transmitted via an ActiveMQ message queue.

methods.name: emitEvent
methods.comment: This method first converts the event into a JSON payload using the MappingJackson2MessageConverter that was set up in the MessagingConfiguration class. It then sends this payload to the ActiveMQ queue with the given queue name.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.CustomerDecisionMessageConsumer
comment: Transport-agnostic handler. Called by the Modulith event listener. No JMS, no remote calls, no cross-module messaging.

methods.name: respond
methods.comment: This is the missing step in your modulith. It moves REQUEST_SUBMITTED -> QUOTE_RECEIVED (or REQUEST_REJECTED), then publishes InsuranceQuoteResponded for CustomerSelfService.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.InsuranceQuoteRequestMessageConsumer
comment: InsuranceQuoteRequestMessageConsumer is a Spring component that consumes InsuranceQuoteRequestEvents as they arrive through the ActiveMQ message queue. It processes these events by creating corresponding InsuranceQuoteRequestAggregateRoot instances.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.PolicyInformationHolder
comment: This REST controller gives clients access to the insurance policies. It is an example of the Information Holder Resource pattern. This particular one is a special type of information holder called Master Data Holder.

methods.name: getPolicy
methods.comment:
Returns the policy for the given policy id.

The query parameter {@code expand } allows clients to provide a so-called Wish List. By default, getPolicy() returns the following response: 
  GET http://localhost:8090/policies/h3riovf4xq/  { "_expandable" : [ "customer" ], "policyId" : "h3riovf4xq", "customer" : "rgpp0wkpec", "creationDate" : "2018-06-19T12:45:46.743+0000", ... }
The response includes only the customer's id. The {@code _expandable } section indicates that the {@code customer } resource can be expanded: 
  GET http://localhost:8090/policies/h3riovf4xq/?expand=customer  { "_expandable" : [ "customer" ], "policyId" : "h3riovf4xq", "customer" : { "customerId" : "rgpp0wkpec", "customerProfile" : { "firstname" : "Max", "lastname" : "Mustermann", ... }, ... }, "creationDate" : "2018-06-19T12:45:46.743+0000", ... }

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.RiskComputationService
comment: This REST controller allows clients to compute the risk factor for a given customer. It is an application of the Computation Function pattern. A Computation Service performs a function f that only depends on its input parameters and does not alter the state of the server.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.configuration.DefaultAuthenticatedRestTemplateClient
comment: Provides a RestTemplate that can be injected into other components. The RestTemplate uses the configured API Key when making a request.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.configuration.MessagingConfiguration
comment: The MessagingConfiguration class configures the ActiveMQ message broker. This broker is used to send events to the Risk Management Server when a policy changes.

methods.name: jacksonJmsMessageConverter
methods.comment: The @Bean attribute turns the returned object (in this case a MessageConverter) into a Spring bean. A bean is an object that is instantiated, assembled, and otherwise managed by a Spring container. Other classes can use dependency injection (e.g., using the @Autowired annotation) to obtain a reference to a specific bean.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.configuration.SwaggerConfiguration
comment: The SwaggerConfiguration class configures the HTTP resource API documentation that is generated by Springfox.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.configuration.WebConfiguration
comment: The WebConfiguration class is used to customize the default Spring MVC configuration.

methods.name: h2servletRegistration
methods.comment: This web servlet makes the web console of the H2 database engine available at the "/console" endpoint.

methods.name: shallowETagHeaderFilter
methods.comment: This is a filter that generates an ETag value based on the content of the response. This ETag is compared to the If-None-Match header of the request. If these headers are equal, the response content is not sent, but rather a 304 "Not Modified" status instead.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.configuration.logging.RequestTracingFilter
comment: This RequestTracingFilter class generates a new random ID for each request and stores it in the MDC (Mapped Diagnostic Context). The ID will then appear in each log entry that is logged as a result of the corresponding request. This can help correlate different log entries during debugging.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.converters.StringToCustomerIdDtoConverter
comment: This converter class allows us to use CustomerIdDto as the type of a @PathVariable parameter in a Spring @RestController class.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.converters.StringToPolicyIdConverter
comment: This converter class allows us to use PolicyId as the type of a @PathVariable parameter in a Spring @RestController class.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.CustomerCoreNotAvailableException
comment: You might be wondering why this Exception class is in the dtos package. Exceptions of this type are thrown whenever the Policy Management backend can't connect to the Customer Core. Spring will then convert this exception into an HTTP 502 response.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.UnknownCustomerException
comment: You might be wondering why this Exception class is in the dtos package. Exceptions of this type are thrown whenever the client tries to create a policy for an unknown customer. Spring will then convert this exception into an HTTP 424 response.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.customer.AddressDto
comment: AddressDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents the postal address of a customer.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.customer.CustomerDto
comment:
The CustomerDto class is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents a single customer. It inherits from the ResourceSupport class which allows us to create a REST representation (e.g., JSON, XML) that follows the HATEOAS principle. For example, links can be added to the representation (e.g., self, address.change) which means that future actions the client may take can be discovered from the resource representation. The CustomerDto is part of the API contract (see the getCustomer() method in @CustomerInformationHolder). In the Swagger API docs (http://localhost:8090/v2/api-docs) generated by SpringFox the is represented by the following JSON Schema. JSON Schema (https://json-schema.org/) is an IETF draft to define the schema of JSON documents: 
  { "title":"CustomerDto", "type":"object", "properties":{ // Note that this _links section of the JSON Schema appears here, because CustomerDto // inherits from ResourceSupport which can add HATEOAS links to the JSON representation // of the resource. "_links":{ "type":"array", "xml":{ "name":"link", "attribute":false, "wrapped":false }, "items":{ "$ref":"#/definitions/Link" } }, "birthday":{ "type":"string", "format":"date-time" }, "city":{ "type":"string" }, "customerId":{ "type":"string" }, "email":{ "type":"string" }, "firstname":{ "type":"string" }, "lastname":{ "type":"string" }, "moveHistory":{ "type":"array", "items":{ "$ref":"#/definitions/AddressDto" } }, "phoneNumber":{ "type":"string" }, "postalCode":{ "type":"string" }, "streetAddress":{ "type":"string" } } }

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.customer.CustomerIdDto
comment: CustomerIdDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents the unique ID of a customer.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.customer.CustomerNotFoundException
comment: You might be wondering why this Exception class is in the dtos package. Exceptions of this type are thrown whenever the client sends a request with an invalid customer id. Spring will then convert this exception into an HTTP 404 response.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.customer.CustomerProfileDto
comment: CustomerProfileDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents the personal data (customer profile) of a customer.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.customer.CustomersDto
comment: The CustomersDto class is a data transfer object (DTO) that contains a list of customers. It inherits from the ResourceSupport class which allows us to create a REST representation (e.g., JSON, XML) that follows the HATEOAS principle. For example, links can be added to the representation (e.g., self, next, prev) which means that future actions the client may take can be discovered from the resource representation.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.customer.PaginatedCustomerResponseDto
comment: The PaginatedCustomerResponseDto holds a collection of CustomerDto with additional metadata parameters such as the limit, offset and size that are used in the Pagination pattern, specifically the Offset-Based Pagination variant.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.insurancequoterequest.CustomerInfoDto
comment: CustomerInfoDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents the customer infos that are part of an Insurance Quote Request.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceOptionsDto
comment: InsuranceOptionsDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that contains the insurance options (e.g., start date, insurance type, etc.) that a customer selected for an Insurance Quote Request.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceQuoteDto
comment: InsuranceQuoteDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents an Insurance Quote which has been submitted as a response to a specific Insurance Quote Request.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceQuoteRequestDto
comment: InsuranceQuoteRequestDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents a request by a customer for a new insurance quote.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceQuoteRequestNotFoundException
comment: You might be wondering why this Exception class is in the dtos package. Exceptions of this type are thrown whenever the client tries to fetch an insurance quote request that doesn't exist. Spring will then convert this exception into an HTTP 404 response.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.insurancequoterequest.InsuranceQuoteResponseDto
comment: InsuranceQuoteResponseDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that contains Lakeside Mutual's response to a specific insurance quote request.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.insurancequoterequest.RequestStatusChangeDto
comment: RequestStatusChangeDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents a status change of an insurance quote request.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.policy.CreatePolicyRequestDto
comment: CreatePolicyRequestDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that is sent to the Policy Management backend when a Lakeside Mutual employee creates a new policy for a customer.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.policy.InsuringAgreementDto
comment: InsuringAgreementDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents the insuring agreement between a customer and Lakeside Mutual.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.policy.InsuringAgreementItemDto
comment: InsuringAgreementItemDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that is used to represent a single item in an insuring agreement.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.policy.MoneyAmountDto
comment: MoneyAmountDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents an amount of money in a specific currency.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.policy.PaginatedPolicyResponseDto
comment: The PaginatedPolicyResponseDto holds a collection of PolicyDto with additional metadata parameters such as the limit, offset and size that are used in the Pagination pattern, specifically the Offset-Based Pagination variant.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.policy.PolicyDto
comment: The PolicyDto class is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents a single insurance policy. It inherits from the ResourceSupport class which allows us to create a REST representation (e.g., JSON, XML) that follows the HATEOAS principle. For example, links can be added to the representation (e.g., self, address.change) which means that future actions the client may take can be discovered from the resource representation.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.policy.PolicyNotFoundException
comment: You might be wondering why this Exception class is in the dtos package. Exceptions of this type are thrown whenever the client sends a request with an invalid policy id. Spring will then convert this exception into an HTTP 404 response.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.policy.PolicyPeriodDto
comment: PolicyPeriodDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that represents the period during which a policy is valid.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.risk.RiskFactorRequestDto
comment: RiskFactorRequestDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that is sent to the Policy Management backend when a client wants to compute the risk factor of a specific customer.

name: com.lakesidemutual.policymanagement.interfaces.dtos.risk.RiskFactorResponseDto
comment: RiskFactorResponseDto is a data transfer object (DTO) that contains the risk factor that was computed for a specific customer.
